_meta.title: Spec 35 optimal-control registry — input-type slice (11 rows)
excluded: system-internal: className — 1 attr (sgs/button), WP wrapper plumbing (block-editor-assigned CSS class name), not a client-facing setting; no editor control to design

rows.label: Choice (single-select from a fixed set)

optimal_control.component: ToggleGroupControl (2–5 short options) OR ComboboxControl (>~10 options, searchable)

props.custom_path: when the underlying CSS/behaviour value is open-ended (not a closed vocabulary), add a 'Custom' option that reveals a free text/colour/unit field — never truncate the option set

optimal_control.preset_layer: n/a — the enum members ARE the presets; no separate custom-box layer unless a Custom-path escape exists (dimension 5)
optimal_control.responsive_device_tier: case-by-case: layout/typography/sizing enums (e.g. direction, layoutMode, alignment) get the 768/1024 switcher; identity/content/behaviour enums (e.g. dataSource, orderBy, conditionalOperator) do not
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ToggleGroupControl (2–5) or Combobox (>~10); every valid option present + Custom path where the underlying value is open-ended — dimension 5 is THE defining dimension for this input-type
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset (Combobox) or a documented default selection (ToggleGroupControl); lives in the relevant ToolsPanel

rows.spec_ref: Part B 'Selection' row (ToggleGroupControl 2–5 / ComboboxControl >~10 / FormTokenField multi-value) + Part H 'segmented choice → ToggleGroupControl · long/searchable list → ComboboxControl' + Part F 'incomplete option sets (Small/Medium, no Custom)'
rows.current_state: 127 attrs across ~60 blocks. full_classification 'why' is almost uniformly 'enum_values present or SelectControl/ToggleGroupControl' — the reclassifier could not distinguish WHICH component renders each attr from static analysis, only that it's a closed-set choice. A handful of entries name the concrete UI need explicitly: menuFallback/conditionalField ('choose from a dynamic list — a select, not free text'), filterTaxonomy/postType/orderBy ('fixed option set'), thicknessUnit ('CSS-unit companion select — same UNIT-COMPANION pattern as every other responsive *Unit attr').
rows.divergence: Cannot be scored precisely without a per-block component audit (the source data conflates SelectControl and ToggleGroupControl under one bucket) — but the pattern strongly implies a mix of bare native <select> (SelectControl) for both short (2–5) and long lists, which fails dimension 5's split rule; several attrs (dataSource, sourcePlatform, videoSource) look CSS/behaviour-adjacent with no visible Custom escape hatch.
rows.notes: Generic enum control only — enum-select members that gate a recurring interaction pattern (e.g. carousel vs grid layoutMode, tabStyle, accordion style) are refined by the behaviour-family slice, not here; this row states the baseline control shape every one of the 127 must clear. A structural per-block audit (component name + option count) is a prerequisite to close the divergence precisely — the reclassifier's static heuristic can't tell ToggleGroupControl from SelectControl from source alone.

rows.label: On/off switch

optimal_control.component: ToggleControl (default) or ToggleGroupControl for a binary segmented choice with two meaningfully-labelled states (e.g. 'Grid'/'List' rather than a bare on/off)

props.help: aria-describedby-linked hint text where the effect is non-obvious

optimal_control.responsive_device_tier: case-by-case: layout/visibility toggles that are meaningfully different per device (hideOnMobile/hideOnTablet, wrap/wrapTablet/wrapMobile) already carry per-tier companion attrs and should route through ResponsiveControl rather than 3 standalone booleans; content/behaviour toggles (autoplay, required, dismissible) do not
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: a boolean's 'reset' is its documented default state; ToolsPanelItem where it lives in a dense panel

rows.spec_ref: Part E2 (ToggleGroupControl selected/focus must be visible under High-Contrast, not colour alone; help via aria-describedby) + Part H 'segmented choice → ToggleGroupControl'. ToggleControl itself is the WP-core baseline for a boolean attr and is not separately enumerated as a Part B/H line — it is the unnamed default every other Part B/H entry assumes when a setting is a plain flag.
rows.current_state: 121 attrs across ~57 blocks. full_classification uniformly reports 'attr_type=boolean' with no component detail — the attr TYPE is boolean, which is consistent with ToggleControl already being the rendering default (WP's own attribute-to-control heuristics favour ToggleControl for boolean attrs), but this is inferred, not confirmed per block.
rows.divergence: Likely low for the base control (ToggleControl is the path of least resistance for a boolean attr and SGS blocks have no reason to hand-roll a checkbox), but three concrete patterns need a per-block check: (1) responsive-tier companions (wrapMobile/wrapTablet/directionMobile pattern) that should route through ResponsiveControl rather than exist as 3 loose booleans; (2) toggles whose two states are really a segmented CHOICE (e.g. a hypothetical grid/list flag) miscast as a boolean instead of ToggleGroupControl; (3) missing aria-describedby help text on non-obvious toggles (Part E2).
rows.notes: Boolean-toggle members that gate a recurring interaction (autoplay+pauseOnHover+showArrows+showDots as a 'carousel controls' cluster, or showDate+showAuthor+showCategory as a 'post-grid meta' cluster) are refined by the behaviour-family slice, not here — this row is the generic control answer only.

rows.label: Text content (heading, label, body copy, placeholder)

optimal_control.component: RichText (inline formatted prose — quote/testimonial/description body) / TextControl (single-line label/name/short string) / TextareaControl (multi-line unformatted copy — help text, success message)
optimal_control.multi_value: none for a single string; excludeKeywords ('comma-separated keyword-filter string') is a MULTI-VALUE violation — should be FormTokenField, not a delimited string in a text box (dimension 4)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: clears to empty string / block.json default

rows.spec_ref: Part F anti-pattern list ('raw-px spacing instead of the token scale' is the closest named text-input anti-pattern; RichText/TextControl/TextareaControl split itself is not a separate Part B/H line — it is the standard WP native component triage assumed by every content-editing field in the spec) + Part A 'element-first panels' (a text field belongs to the specific inner element it edits, not a generic 'Content' catch-all)
rows.current_state: 74 attrs across ~40 blocks. full_classification is dominated by the single generic label 'prose/label copy — role=text-content, Text(area)Control, or canonical_slot=text/content' for the large majority — the reclassifier cannot distinguish single-line labels from multi-line body copy from source alone. A few attrs are called out with a distinct shape: anchor ('HTML id/slug string'), excludeKeywords ('comma-separated keyword-filter string'), ratingScaleMax ('numeric scale-ceiling stored as a string'), schemaItemName ('Schema.org item-name string').
rows.divergence: The RichText/TextControl/TextareaControl split by sub-role cannot be scored from this source alone — needs a per-attr audit against each block's edit.js. Two concrete issues ARE visible in the data: excludeKeywords is a comma-delimited string that should be FormTokenField (dimension 4 violation); ratingScaleMax is a numeric value mis-typed as a string attr (attr_type mismatch, not a control mismatch).
rows.notes: British English in all label copy this control produces. Body/description-length fields (bio, description, message, successMessage) should default to TextareaControl or RichText, not a cramped single-line TextControl — the per-block audit should check for this specific failure mode (a multi-sentence value crammed into a one-line input).

rows.label: Numeric value (count, size, offset, timing)

optimal_control.component: RangeControl (bounded value — real min/max/step, +input field +reset) for dimensionless counts/percentages/speeds; UnitControl for anything carrying an implicit length/pixel/ms meaning
optimal_control.units: px-shaped attrs (thickness/thicknessTablet/thicknessMobile, positionX/positionY + tier companions, collapseCustomPx, iconCircleSize, contentIconSize, ctaPaddingX/ctaPaddingY, scrollOffset) need a real unit, not a bare number — dimension 2
optimal_control.responsive_device_tier: already-present tier companions (iconSizeMobile/iconSizeTablet, positionXMobile/Tablet, positionYMobile/Tablet, rotationMobile/Tablet, thicknessMobile/Tablet) confirm this input-type is device-tier-worthy and should route through ResponsiveControl rather than 3 loose sibling attrs
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset + resetFallbackValue on every RangeControl (Part B 'RangeControl' row); ToolsPanel where dense (e.g. sgs/decorative-image's position/rotation/parallax cluster)

rows.spec_ref: Part B 'RangeControl' row (min/max/step real; withInputField; allowReset+resetFallbackValue; unit shown — incomplete smell: slider only, no input/reset, arbitrary 0–100) + Part B 'UnitControl' row (units covering every meaningful unit; isResetValueOnUnitChange — incomplete smell: px-only) + Part H 'numeric+unit → UnitControl · bounded numeric → RangeControl (+input+reset)'
rows.current_state: 65 attrs across ~28 blocks. full_classification reports only 'attr_type number/integer or Range/NumberControl' for every entry — no bound/unit/reset detail is recoverable from source alone. Several attrs are self-evidently pixel-shaped by name (thickness*, positionX/Y*, collapseCustomPx, iconCircleSize, contentIconSize) and already carry Tablet/Mobile sibling attrs, confirming they are responsive-worthy.
rows.divergence: The generic label 'Range/NumberControl' is itself the smell Part B calls out — 'Range/NumberControl' undifferentiated suggests some of these 65 are bare NumberControl inputs (no slider, no bounds shown) rather than a real RangeControl with min/max/step/input/reset; and pixel-shaped attrs almost certainly render as bare numbers with no unit suffix at all (dimension 2 gap) rather than UnitControl.
rows.notes: This is the input-type most exposed to Bean's CUSTOM-BOX-PLUS-PRESET and UNITS dimensions (1 and 2) — a RangeControl with no real min/max or a plain number field with no unit is exactly the FAIL smell Part B names. Recommend the per-block audit prioritise this group first: it's the cheapest fix (swap NumberControl → RangeControl/UnitControl) with the highest volume (65 attrs).

rows.label: Image / video / audio media

optimal_control.component: MediaUpload + MediaUploadCheck (single item); MediaGalleryPicker (array/repeated media — multiple='add' + gallery)
optimal_control.responsive_device_tier: device-specific logo/background variants (desktopLogoId/tabletLogoId/mobileLogoId, backgroundImage/backgroundImageTablet/backgroundImageMobile, bgVideo/bgVideoMobile) are the media-equivalent of a responsive attribute — should route through the same 768/1024 device-tier switcher pattern rather than 3 parallel named attrs the operator must remember to fill separately
optimal_control.multi_value: gallery: images, logos, mediaItems are array attrs and need MediaGalleryPicker; corner-note — logoSwitchMode/logoSwitchCustomPx/logoFit are companion settings that ride alongside the picker, not separate media-type controls
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: MediaUpload's own 'Remove'/clear affordance; array pickers need a per-item remove + drag-reorder

rows.spec_ref: Part B 'Media/gallery' row (multiple='add' + gallery + array attr + MediaUploadCheck + drag-drop — incomplete smell: scalar attr + single MediaUpload) + Part I 'Bulk media/gallery — MISSING (MediaPicker single) → BUILD MediaGalleryPicker' (Wave 2, #5) + Part C Media (focal point/aspect-ratio/object-fit T)
rows.current_state: 44 attrs across 16 blocks. full_classification labels most as 'role=image-object or media/logo/avatar/video attr name' (a single scalar media reference) and 3 explicitly as array/repeater-shaped: images (sgs/gallery), logos (sgs/brand-strip), mediaItems (sgs/gallery). Several attrs are fragmented facets of ONE logical media object rather than a single {id,url,alt} shape — e.g. sgs/media splits imageId/imageUrl/imageAlt into 3 sibling attrs instead of one object attr; sgs/audio splits audioId/audioUrl/audioMimeType similarly.
rows.divergence: The array attrs (images, logos, mediaItems) confirm Part I's finding — MediaGalleryPicker is a framework-wide MISSING component, so these 3 (44 total member count includes their per-item companions like showNames/fadeEdges from other groups) are very likely still on a single MediaUpload with no bulk/gallery affordance. The scalar-but-fragmented attrs (imageId+imageUrl+imageAlt as 3 separate top-level attrs rather than one object) violate the object-attr shape named in the design spine's worked url-link example and Bean's dedup-by-identity lesson.
rows.notes: Two distinct fixes bundled under one setting_key: (1) build MediaGalleryPicker and roll it out to the 3 array attrs (Wave 2 #5, already a named framework gap); (2) consolidate the scalar id/url/alt/mimeType triples into a single object attr per media slot, matching the {id,url,alt,...} shape Part B implies. Focal point / size / aspect-ratio (Part C) apply where the media is a still image — cross-reference sgs/decorative-image and sgs/media which already carry imageControls per CLAUDE.md's 'Image controls discipline'.

rows.label: Structured repeater data (items, options, steps)

optimal_control.component: A structured per-item repeater panel — an ordered list of item cards, each expandable to its own mini-form of typed sub-fields (text/media/link/colour per item shape), with add/remove/drag-reorder — never a raw JSON textarea

props.item_card: collapsed summary row (drag handle, item label, remove button) that expands to the item's own typed controls

optimal_control.preset_layer: an empty/new item seeds from a documented default shape, never a blank object the operator must know how to fill
optimal_control.units: n/a — the repeater's individual sub-fields carry their own unit/colour/media rules per their own input-type
optimal_control.multi_value: the entire setting_key IS the multi-value case — array of structured objects, not a scalar; this is the array/object equivalent of MULTI-VALUE dimension 4
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: n/a at the repeater level; individual item sub-fields (e.g. a per-step icon or per-plan CTA style) carry their own dropdown-completeness rule
optimal_control.alpha_clearable: n/a at the repeater level; per-item colour sub-fields (if any) need it
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: remove-item + 'clear all' are the reset path; no single default value to reset TO since it's a list

rows.spec_ref: Part B 'Media/gallery' row's array-attr principle extended to non-media repeaters (array attr + structured item UI, not a scalar) + Part I 'Bulk media/gallery — BUILD MediaGalleryPicker' is the media-specific sibling of this same repeater pattern + Part F anti-pattern 'bespoke Custom CSS field on the block' (the raw-JSON-textarea failure mode this row rules out)
rows.current_state: 25 attrs across 20 blocks. full_classification labels every member identically: 'attr_type array/object holding structured repeater/config data, not a media object' — this confirms the attrs are genuinely array/object-shaped (good — not flattened into 20 scalar attrs), but gives no visibility into whether the CURRENT editor UI is a real per-item repeater panel or a raw JSON/serialised-text editing experience.
rows.divergence: Cannot be scored precisely without a per-block UI audit — the data proves the attr TYPE is already correct (array/object, not flattened scalars, which is the main MULTI-VALUE win) but not whether the CONTROL is a proper repeater panel. Given these are core SGS composite blocks (card-grid items, pricing-table plans, process-steps steps, form-field-select options) which are among the most mature blocks in the framework, a real repeater panel is plausible for most — but this is an assumption, not a measurement.
rows.notes: AMBIGUOUS INPUT-TYPE (flagged per report instructions): json-config is the one group where 'optimal component' cannot be stated with confidence from the source data alone, because a repeater panel is a UI/UX judgement call (per-item field set, disclosure depth) that varies by what each item actually IS (a pricing plan has different fields than a timeline entry) — Spec 35 Parts B/H describe the array-attr PRINCIPLE but no single named component covers 'repeater panel', unlike every other input-type row which maps to one concrete WP/SGS component.

rows.label: Colour (hover-state / override variant)

optimal_control.preset_layer: token swatches (theme.json palette) seed the picker; a custom hex/rgba value is always still editable underneath
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: clearable resets to unset (inherits base/no override), distinct from alpha-0 (Part B Colour row: alpha-0 ≠ unset)

rows.spec_ref: Part B 'Colour' row (enableAlpha ≈always + clearable, alpha-0 ≠ unset; disableCustomColors false) + Part I 'Colour — DesignTokenPicker EXISTS — NO enableAlpha/clearable audit → ADD enableAlpha + clearable (framework-wide transparent fix)' + Part J Wave 1 #1 (highest-priority item in the whole roadmap) + D302 (emit hex8 so WP safecss doesn't strip functional rgba)
rows.current_state: 20 attrs across 20 blocks (all Hover-state or override colour variants — e.g. colourTextHover, ctaColourBorderHover, overlayGradientFrom/To, gradientColourStart/End). full_classification confirms every single member is already 'DesignTokenPicker colour-token picker (Hover-state or override variant of a standard colour input)' — the CORRECT component is already in use everywhere.
rows.divergence: Component choice is already correct (DesignTokenPicker, matching the optimal control exactly) — the gap is entirely the enableAlpha/clearable AUDIT named in Part I as a still-open framework-wide fix, not a component swap. This is the one input-type row where 'current_state' and 'optimal_control' agree on the component and diverge only on props.
rows.notes: Despite the component already matching, severity is HIGH because Part J ranks the enableAlpha+clearable fix as Wave-1 item #1 (highest framework-wide priority, 'fixes transparent everywhere') and D302 requires hex8 emission so WP's safecss_filter_attr doesn't silently strip functional rgba() on the frontend — a correctness bug, not just a UX gap. These are Hover-state/override colours specifically (not base colours), so StateToggleControl (Part I, EXISTS) should also wrap the Normal/Hover pairing rather than exposing 20 always-visible separate Hover fields.

rows.label: Icon (dashicon / Lucide / emoji / WP icon)

optimal_control.component: The SGS icon picker — a single identity control with an internal source switch (dashicon/lucide-wp-icon/emoji), search-as-you-type, and a live preview swatch
optimal_control.preset_layer: the icon library IS the preset set; no separate custom-box layer beyond the identity picker itself
optimal_control.multi_value: icons (sgs/social-icons, plural) is an array of icon-identity objects and needs the repeater treatment (see input:json-config), not a scalar icon control
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: n/a — search-driven, not a closed dropdown

rows.spec_ref: Part H 'compact secondary → Dropdown/DropdownMenu' (the picker's own popover shape) — icon-identity itself is not a separately named Part B/H/I line; it is inferred from lucide-icons.php's existing icon roster (plugins/sgs-blocks/includes/lucide-icons.php) plus the dedup-by-identity-not-name principle applied to source-fragmented attrs below
rows.current_state: 17 attrs across 12 blocks. full_classification labels all as 'icon-identity picker (slug/dashicon/emoji/wp-icon), not a media object' — but the member list itself reveals a fragmentation pattern: sgs/separator carries FIVE parallel attrs for what should be ONE icon identity — contentIconSource, contentIconDashicon, contentIconEmoji, contentIconName, contentIconWpIcon (one active value + 4 dormant siblings depending on source). sgs/icon similarly splits iconSource/dashiconName/emojiChar/wpIconName/iconName across 5 attrs for one logical icon.
rows.divergence: This is the dedup-by-identity failure the project's own MEMORY.md names directly ('944 attrs → 0 truly-unique... classify by SEMANTIC IDENTITY not the name'): each icon setting is stored as N parallel scalar attrs (one per possible source) instead of ONE identity object ({source, value}) resolved by a single picker with an internal source switch. This is a genuine control-shape gap, not just a props gap.
rows.notes: icons (plural, sgs/social-icons) needs the repeater treatment on top of the identity-object fix — each repeater item is one icon-identity object, not another flat scalar. showIcon (business-info, notice-banner) is a boolean gate for whether the icon shows at all and belongs with input:boolean-toggle, not this row's control design, though it's correctly grouped here as a member of the icon-family attr set.

rows.label: Link / URL
rows.spec_ref: Part B 'Link/CTA' row (LinkControl: internal search + new-tab + rel nofollow/sponsored via settings — incomplete smell: raw URL TextControl) + Part C Links (internal-content search T, new-tab+auto-rel-noopener T, rel nofollow/sponsored T) + Part F 'raw URL field instead of LinkControl' + Part G (LinkControl over raw URL text fields, HIGH priority) + Part I 'Link/CTA — likely raw URL fields → AUDIT + migrate to LinkControl wrapper' + Part J Wave 1 #2
rows.current_state: 15 attrs across 12 blocks — matches the design spine's own worked example exactly (blockLink, businessUnitUrl, cta2Url, ctaPrimaryUrl, ctaSecondaryUrl, ctaUrl, indexVariationUrl, linkEmail, linkPhone, linkTarget, linkUrl, phoneNumber, reviewRequestUrl, sgsBlockLink, url). full_classification labels every member 'URL/href/link-target field' uniformly — consistent with raw TextControl/string attrs rather than SgsLinkControl's object shape.
rows.divergence: Raw URL fields lack internal search + new-tab + rel; the flat url/blockLink/ctaUrl string attrs need to become object shape {url, opensInNewTab, rel} to carry LinkControl's settings. linkEmail/linkPhone/phoneNumber are semantically constrained (mailto:/tel:) and should validate/prefix accordingly rather than accept an arbitrary URL — a sub-case LinkControl doesn't cover natively and needs a thin wrapper rule.
rows.notes: This row is copied near-verbatim from the design spine's own worked example (spec-35-setting-registry-design.md) — the source counts (15 attrs / 12 blocks) match exactly, confirming the worked example already reflects this exact group. Converter-population note: any cloned block that fills these attrs must emit the {url,opensInNewTab,rel} object shape, not a flat string, per the object-typed-attr-coerces-flat-to-default gotcha in MEMORY.md (D328) — a flat string emit would be silently coerced to the block.json default at render.

rows.label: Date / datetime value

optimal_control.reset_disclosure: clear-to-none affordance; countdown-timer's targetDate needs a sane default (e.g. +7 days) rather than blank

rows.current_state: 4 attrs across 3 blocks: maxDate/minDate (sgs/form-field-date), reviewDate (sgs/testimonial), targetDate (sgs/countdown-timer). full_classification labels all uniformly 'date/datetime value' with no component detail.
rows.divergence: No component evidence recoverable from source; the naming pattern (raw camelCase date attrs with no companion 'DateFormat'/'DateTimezone' attrs) is consistent with either a real DateTimePicker or a plain native <input type='date'> TextControl-equivalent — needs a per-block audit to confirm. sgs/form-field-date's maxDate/minDate are a bounds PAIR (like RangeControl's min/max) and should be edited together in one min/max UI, not as two independent unrelated pickers.
rows.notes: Small group (4 attrs) — cheap to audit and fix directly rather than needing a framework-wide sweep. targetDate (countdown-timer) is timezone-sensitive (the block already has evergreenMode/evergreenHours/evergreenMinutes as an alternative relative-countdown mode) — the picker should make clear whether the date is stored as UTC or site-local.

rows.label: Raw SVG markup (decorative background shape)

optimal_control.component: TextareaControl (monospace font) for authoring/pasting the markup, gated by mandatory SVG-sanitise-on-upload before the value is ever stored or rendered

props.sanitise: server-side SVG sanitiser (strip <script>, on* handlers, external refs) runs BEFORE the attribute is saved — never trust raw client input

optimal_control.preset_layer: a small curated set of built-in decorative shapes (wave/blob/angle) seeds the field; pasting custom markup is the escape hatch, always sanitised

rows.spec_ref: Part C Media 'SVG sanitise-on-upload (security — never raw) T' (table-stakes, explicitly named security requirement) — TextareaControl itself is not a separate Part B/H line but is the correct WP-native raw-markup editing component given the value is genuinely code, not prose
rows.current_state: 2 attrs across 5 blocks: bgSvgContent (sgs/container, sgs/cta-section, sgs/hero) and svgContent (sgs/hero, sgs/media). full_classification labels both 'raw SVG markup string' with no sanitisation detail visible from source.
rows.divergence: SECURITY GAP, not just a UX gap — Part C names SVG sanitise-on-upload as table-stakes (never trust raw markup) and there is no evidence in the source data that either attr passes through a sanitiser before being stored or rendered (both are used as background decoration, i.e. rendered inline into the page, which is exactly the XSS-risk path the CLAUDE.md security non-negotiables and Part C both flag). This must be verified against render.php for sgs/container, sgs/cta-section, sgs/hero, sgs/media directly, not assumed safe.
rows.notes: Smallest member count (2 attrs) but flagged HIGH severity purely on security grounds per CLAUDE.md's non-negotiable 'no OWASP top 10' + Part C's explicit sanitise-on-upload requirement — raw SVG rendered inline is a known XSS vector (embedded <script>/event-handler attributes) regardless of how polished the editor control is. Verifying/adding server-side sanitisation (e.g. wp_kses with an SVG-safe tag allowlist, or a dedicated SVG sanitiser library) should be treated as a P0 fix independent of the Phase-3 lint rollout.
